Submodules

api.permissions module

class api.permissions.CanReadApprovedArticles[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Object-level rule for article detail: - approved articles: any authenticated user can view - unapproved articles: editors only

has_object_permission(request: Any, view: Any, obj: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'You do not have permission to view this article.'
class api.permissions.IsEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to editors. Supports role field and group fallback.

has_permission(request: Any, view: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'Editor role required.'
class api.permissions.IsJournalist[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to journalists. Supports role field and group fallback.

has_permission(request: Any, view: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'Journalist role required.'
class api.permissions.IsJournalistOrEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access to journalists or editors. Supports role field and group fallback.

has_permission(request: Any, view: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'Journalist or Editor role required.'
class api.permissions.IsOwnerOrEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Object-level permission for resource owners and editors.

has_object_permission(request: Any, view: Any, obj: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'You do not have permission to access this resource.'
class api.permissions.IsOwnerOrEditorForNewsletter[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Object-level permission for Newsletter: - owner/author can modify - editors can modify any newsletter

has_object_permission(request: Any, view: Any, obj: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'You do not have permission to modify this newsletter.'
class api.permissions.IsReader[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allows access only to readers. Supports role field and group fallback.

has_permission(request: Any, view: Any) → bool[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

message = 'Reader role required.'

api.serializers module

class api.serializers.ArticleCreateSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer used when journalists submit new articles.

We do NOT expose author/approved fields to the client: - author is set from request.user - approved is forced to False in the view

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']
model

alias of Article

class api.serializers.ArticleDetailSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Article detail serializer. Includes full content and approval metadata.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'approved', 'created_at', 'approved_at', 'approved_by', 'approved_by_username']
model

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ['author', 'approved', 'created_at', 'approved_at', 'approved_by']
class api.serializers.ArticleListSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Public/article-list serializer. Keeps the response small for list endpoints.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'approved', 'created_at', 'approved_at']
model

alias of Article

class api.serializers.CustomUserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Safe read-only user serializer for API responses. Includes basic identity and role fields only.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role']
model

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role']
class api.serializers.JournalistSubscriptionSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Reader subscription to a journalist.

POST input: - journalist

Read output: - includes usernames for convenience

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'journalist', 'journalist_username', 'reader', 'reader_username', 'created_at']
model

alias of JournalistSubscription

read_only_fields = ['reader', 'reader_username', 'created_at']
class api.serializers.NewsletterCreateUpdateSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Create/update serializer for newsletters.

Allows setting article IDs (many-to-many). Author is set from request.user in the view.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'description', 'articles']
model

alias of Newsletter

class api.serializers.NewsletterDetailSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Detailed serializer for a newsletter, including linked article IDs.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'author_username', 'articles', 'created_at']
model

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ['author', 'created_at']
class api.serializers.NewsletterListSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Lightweight serializer for newsletter list endpoint.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'author_username', 'article_count', 'created_at']
model

alias of Newsletter

class api.serializers.PublisherMembershipSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Read-only serializer for publisher memberships.

Shows which user belongs to which publisher and their membership role (editor/journalist within that publisher).

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'user', 'user_username', 'member_role']
model

alias of PublisherMembership

class api.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Read-only publisher serializer.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'name', 'description', 'created_at']
model

alias of Publisher

class api.serializers.PublisherSubscriptionSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Reader subscription to a publisher.

POST input: - publisher

Read output: - includes publisher name and reader username for convenience

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'reader', 'reader_username', 'created_at']
model

alias of PublisherSubscription

api.urls module

api/urls.py

DRF API routes for the Capstone News app.

api.views module

API views for the Capstone News project.

This module provides endpoints for article management, article approval, subscriptions, newsletters, publishers, and publisher memberships.

class api.views.ApproveArticleAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Editor-only action endpoint to approve an article.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsEditor'>]
post(request: Any, pk: int) → Response[source]

Approve the specified article.

class api.views.ArticleDetailAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Retrieve/update/delete a single article.

GET: - Approved articles: any authenticated user - Unapproved articles: editors only

PUT/PATCH/DELETE: - Owner journalist or editor only

get_object() → Any[source]

Retrieve the article and check permissions.

get_permissions() → list[Any][source]

Return permissions based on request method and article approval status.

get_serializer_class() → Any[source]

Return the serializer used for the current request method.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
queryset = <QuerySet [<Article: article to test email>, <Article: new article>, <Article: 123>]>
class api.views.ArticleListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

GET:

  • Authenticated users can list approved articles only.

POST:

  • Journalists can submit new articles.

  • author is automatically set from request.user

  • approved is forced to False

get_permissions() → list[Any][source]

Return permissions based on whether the request is read or create.

get_queryset() → Any[source]

Return articles available to the current authenticated user.

get_serializer_class() → Any[source]

Return the serializer used for the current request method.

perform_create(serializer: Any) → None[source]

Create a new article for the current journalist as pending approval.

class api.views.JournalistSubscriptionDeleteAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: DestroyAPIView

Reader unsubscribe endpoint for journalist subscriptions.

Readers can only delete their own subscriptions. Editors may delete any (fallback for admin/testing).

get_object() → Any[source]

Retrieve the journalist subscription and check permissions.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsOwnerOrEditor'>]
queryset = <QuerySet [<JournalistSubscription: sergioreader -> Sergiojournalist>]>
serializer_class

alias of JournalistSubscriptionSerializer

class api.views.JournalistSubscriptionListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

Reader subscription endpoint for journalists.

GET: - readers see only their own journalist subscriptions

POST: - readers subscribe to a journalist - reader is set automatically from request.user

get_queryset() → Any[source]

Return the queryset of journalist subscriptions for the current reader.

perform_create(serializer: Any) → None[source]

Create a new journalist subscription for the current reader.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsReader'>]
serializer_class

alias of JournalistSubscriptionSerializer

class api.views.NewsletterDetailAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Retrieve/update/delete a newsletter.

GET: - authenticated users can view (subject to your existing permissions)

PUT/PATCH/DELETE: - owner journalist or editor only

get_object() → Any[source]

Retrieve the newsletter and check permissions.

get_permissions() → list[Any][source]

Return permissions based on request method.

get_serializer_class() → Any[source]

Return the serializer used for the current request method.

queryset = <QuerySet []>
class api.views.NewsletterListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

Newsletters endpoint.

GET: - any authenticated user can list newsletters

POST: - journalists and editors can create newsletters - author is set automatically from request.user

get_permissions() → list[Any][source]

Return permissions based on whether the request is read or create.

get_serializer_class() → Any[source]

Return the serializer used for the current request method.

perform_create(serializer: Any) → None[source]

Create a new newsletter with the current user as the author.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
queryset = <QuerySet []>
class api.views.NewsletterUpdateDeleteAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: RetrieveUpdateDestroyAPIView

Update/delete a newsletter.

Rules: - Author can update/delete own newsletter - Editors can update/delete any newsletter

get_object() → Any[source]

Retrieve the newsletter and check permissions.

get_serializer_class() → Any[source]

Return the serializer used for the current request method.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsOwnerOrEditorForNewsletter'>]
queryset = <QuerySet []>
class api.views.PendingArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListAPIView

Editor-only list of pending articles (approved=False).

get_queryset() → Any[source]

Return the queryset of pending articles for editors.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsEditor'>]
serializer_class

alias of ArticleListSerializer

Retrieve a single publisher (authenticated users).

List all publishers (authenticated users).

class api.views.PublisherMembershipDetailAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: RetrieveAPIView

Retrieve a single publisher membership (authenticated users).

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
queryset = <QuerySet []>
serializer_class

alias of PublisherMembershipSerializer

class api.views.PublisherMembershipListAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListAPIView

List publisher memberships (authenticated users).

Optional filters: - /api/publisher-memberships/?publisher=<id> - /api/publisher-memberships/?user=<id> - /api/publisher-memberships/?member_role=editor

get_queryset() → Any[source]

Return the queryset of publisher memberships with optional filters.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
serializer_class

alias of PublisherMembershipSerializer

class api.views.PublisherSubscriptionDeleteAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: DestroyAPIView

Reader unsubscribe endpoint for publisher subscriptions.

Readers can only delete their own subscriptions. Editors may delete any (fallback for admin/testing).

get_object() → Any[source]

Retrieve the publisher subscription and check permissions.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsOwnerOrEditor'>]
queryset = <QuerySet []>
serializer_class

class api.views.PublisherSubscriptionListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListCreateAPIView

Reader subscription endpoint for publishers.

GET: - readers see only their own publisher subscriptions

POST: - readers subscribe to a publisher - reader is set automatically from request.user

get_queryset() → Any[source]

Return the queryset of publisher subscriptions for the current reader.

perform_create(serializer: Any) → None[source]

Create a new publisher subscription for the current reader.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsReader'>]
serializer_class

alias of PublisherSubscriptionSerializer

class api.views.RejectArticleAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Editor-only action endpoint to reject an article.

Current behavior: delete article (simple version).

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsEditor'>]
post(request: Any, pk: int) → Response[source]

Reject the specified article.

class api.views.SubscribedArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ListAPIView

Returns approved articles from the authenticated reader’s subscribed publishers and subscribed journalists.

get_queryset() → Any[source]

Return the queryset of approved articles from the reader’s subscriptions.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'api.permissions.IsReader'>]
serializer_class

alias of ArticleListSerializer
